claimText: Project Suncatcher is a Google initiative that explores AI computing in space using solar-powered satellites in low-Earth orbit.
evidenceExcerpt: Google launched Project Suncatcher to explore AI computing in space using solar-powered satellites in low-Earth orbit.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent space and AI initiatives.
distractorHooks: Attributing Project Suncatcher to NASA, SpaceX, or ISRO
Stating it operates in geostationary orbit instead of low-Earth orbit

claimText: Ironwood is the 7th-generation Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) developed by Google.
evidenceExcerpt: Google also developed Ironwood, its 7th-generation Tensor Processing Unit (TPU).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching AI hardware codenames to their respective companies.
distractorHooks: Attributing Ironwood to Microsoft, OpenAI, or NVIDIA
Describing Ironwood as a software model rather than a hardware TPU

claimText: A Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) is an Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) specialized for AI model performance.
evidenceExcerpt: A TPU is an Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) specialized for AI model performance.
examUse: Conceptual statement-based question on AI hardware architectures.
distractorHooks: Defining TPU as a general-purpose processor like a CPU
Confusing ASIC with Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs)
